title: Source-backed overlap claim for policy-as-code and policy reference
summary: Proposes that policy-as-code, as represented by Open Policy Agent documentation, should be classified as overlapping with the StegVerse term Policy Reference, not equivalent to it.

claim_comparison.stegverse_term: Policy Reference identifies the specific rule, policy, standard, or governance artifact used to evaluate a transition.
claim_comparison.external_term: Policy-as-code represents and evaluates policy logic in code or structured policy language.

claim_comparison.stegverse_term: Policy Reference must be stable enough to reconstruct which rule basis applied at commit time.
claim_comparison.external_term: OPA evaluates structured input against policies and data, but the documentation does not by itself impose StegVerse commit-time receipt linkage.

claim_comparison.stegverse_term: Policy Reference identifies the governing basis for a transition decision without itself proving authority.
claim_comparison.external_term: Policy-as-code can supply a rule base or decision input but does not by itself define the full StegVerse authority class or consequence boundary.

claim_comparison.stegverse_term: Policy Reference supports reconstructing why a result was governed by a specific rule basis.
claim_comparison.external_term: Policy-as-code can support reproducible evaluation when versions, inputs, and data are preserved.

mismatches: Policy-as-code can be a form of policy implementation, while Policy Reference is a citation of the rule basis used for a transition.
Policy-as-code does not by itself guarantee that the cited rule version, inputs, evidence posture, and review posture are receipt-bound.
Policy-as-code may produce a decision result without proving StegVerse commit-time authority or consequence standing.
risk_of_overclaiming: Calling policy-as-code equivalent to Policy Reference could collapse implementation of policy with citation of policy basis.
Calling policy-as-code equivalent could imply OPA decisions automatically satisfy StegVerse admissibility, which is not claimed.
proposed_text: policy-as-code, when used to represent or evaluate policy logic as code or structured policy language. This overlaps with Policy Reference when the policy artifact supplies the rule basis for a transition, but it is not equivalent unless the specific policy artifact and version are cited as the commit-time rule basis.
non_claims: This proposal does not accept policy-as-code as equivalent to Policy Reference.
This proposal does not claim Open Policy Agent endorses StegVerse terminology.
This proposal does not claim policy-as-code output is sufficient for admissibility.
